title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: A dazzling novel about all the choices that go into a life well lived. Between life and death there is a library, and within that library, the shelves go on forever.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship that teaches you how to write clean, maintainable code. Even bad code can function, but clean code matters.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: A landmark volume in science writing that explores the mysteries of the universe, from the Big Bang to black holes.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A portrait of the Jazz Age in all of its decadence and excess, exploring themes of wealth, love, and the American Dream.

title: JavaScript: The Good Parts
author: Douglas Crockford
description: An in-depth look at JavaScript that separates the good parts from the bad, helping you write more effective code.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A groundbreaking exploration of evolution from the gene's perspective, revolutionizing our understanding of natural selection.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A gripping tale of racial injustice and childhood innocence in the American South during the 1930s.

title: Design Patterns
author: Erich Gamma
description: Capturing a wealth of experience in software design, this book presents 23 patterns that solve common design problems.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: Explores the fifteen billion years of cosmic evolution that have brought us to where we are, from the origins of life to the future of humanity.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian social science fiction novel that explores the consequences of totalitarianism, mass surveillance, and repressive regimentation.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt
description: A guide to creating working, maintainable code that delights users. Covers topics from personal responsibility to architectural techniques.

title: The Origin of Species
author: Charles Darwin
description: Darwin's groundbreaking work on evolutionary biology that introduced the scientific theory of natural selection.
